task_completion.description: Open the automated processing pipeline builder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Graph Builder (in SNAP) or a similar visual workflow/pipeline builder interface is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Load the specified Landsat multispectral imagery into the pipeline.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Read' or input node in the pipeline is configured with the file path '/home/ga/snap_data/landsat_multispectral.tif'.

task_completion.description: Add a spatial subset operation to the pipeline.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Subset' node is present in the processing graph, connected downstream from the Read node, with spatial cropping parameters visible in its configuration tab.

task_completion.description: Add a vegetation-related spectral index computation to the pipeline.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'NDVI' node, or a 'BandMaths' node explicitly configured with a vegetation index formula (e.g., using B4 and B3), is present in the processing graph.

task_completion.description: Save the reusable workflow definition file to the projects directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager shows a workflow file (e.g., an .xml file from Graph Builder) being saved to or existing in '/home/ga/snap_projects/'.

task_completion.description: Configure the output destination and execute the pipeline to save the product.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Write' node is configured to save the output product to '/home/ga/snap_projects/', and there is evidence of execution (e.g., a progress bar, a completion dialog, or the new product appearing in the file manager/Product Explorer).

integrity.description: Agent used the application GUI to build the pipeline.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the remote sensing software's graphical interface (e.g., SNAP Graph Builder) rather than terminal commands mocking the files.

integrity.description: The workflow definition file was generated by the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The workflow file was saved via the software's 'Save Graph' or export functionality, not by manually typing dummy content into a text editor.

integrity.description: The output product was generated by actually running the pipeline.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of the pipeline executing (e.g., 'Executing processing graph' progress bar) is visible, rather than the agent simply copying the input file or using 'touch' to create an empty file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input data is a Landsat 7 image located at /home/ga/snap_data/landsat_multispectral.tif containing 4 bands (B1, B2, B3, B4). A vegetation index (like NDVI) typically requires the Near-Infrared (B4) and Red (B3) bands. In SNAP, automated pipelines are created using the 'Graph Builder' tool, which saves reusable workflow definitions as XML files.
